mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in industrial production over the next 5 trading days signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 supply shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over the next 60 trading days signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 labor market shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts over the next 20 trading days signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 housing market shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real disposable personal income over the next 5 trading days signals a strengthening consumer economy that boosts equity demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening consumer periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 consumer shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.2% increase in average hourly earnings over the next 60 trading days signals a strengthening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 labor market shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the consumer price index over the next 20 trading days signals a strengthening inflationary environment that boosts interest rates and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 inflation shock.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% decline in industrial production over the next 60 trading days signals a weakening supply chain that boosts corporate costs and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism fails to capture the 2020 COVID-19 supply shock.
